name: Feature Request / New Level
about: Suggest a new level, command, or improvement
title: "[Feature] "
labels: enhancement
assignees: ''
---

## Type of Request

- [ ] New level
- [ ] New command / feature
- [ ] Improvement to existing level
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Other

---

## Description

**What are you proposing?**

**Why does NEXUS need this?**
<!-- What skill gap does it fill, or what bug/limitation does it fix? -->

---

## For New Level Proposals

**Skill taught:**
<!-- What specific Linux/security command or concept does this teach? -->

**Code name:**
<!-- e.g. PHANTOM_SIGNAL, THE_MAZE -->

**Difficulty tier:**
- [ ] Beginner (L01–L04)
- [ ] Intermediate (L05–L12)
- [ ] Advanced (L13–L20)

**Bypass resistance:**
<!-- How does the level prevent cat *, wildcard abuse, ls -laR shortcuts? -->

**Real-world relevance:**
<!-- Where would this skill appear in a real CTF, pentest, or recon workflow? -->

**Cross-platform:**
- [ ] Tested on Termux
- [ ] Tested on Linux
- [ ] Uses only POSIX-safe tools (no GNU-only flags without fallback)

---

## For Feature/Command Proposals

**Proposed command or behavior:**

**Example usage:**
```bash
bash wargame.sh <your-command>
```

**Does this break existing functionality?**
- [ ] No
- [ ] Yes — explain: ___________

---

## Alternatives Considered

<!-- Any other approaches you thought about? -->
